project.name: Credit Governance & Budget Enforcement — Hardening + Subscription Layer
project.context: The credit consumption side is production-grade (37+ stories shipped). This plan closes the remaining governance gaps: graceful fleet degradation, Basic plan bundled credits, and subscription management UI. References but does not duplicate plan-billing-completeness-audit.json (GAP-001–010) which covers unwired edge functions and CORS fixes.

features.name: Graceful Fleet Degradation
features.description: When credits run low during autonomous agent loops, complete the current action before stopping. Currently throws CreditExhaustedError mid-loop which aborts ungracefully. User gets notified that the agent paused.

features.name: Basic Plan Bundled Credits + Subscription Flow
features.description: Wire bundled credits for Basic (£29/month) plan. Pro (250 credits) already works. Basic needs its own credit allocation on creation, renewal, and upgrade. Also verify the self-serve subscription checkout flow works end-to-end.

features.name: Subscription Management UI Polish
features.description: BillingSettingsPage exists but the subscription management experience needs polish: current plan display, upgrade/downgrade flow, cancel with credit expiry warning, and billing portal integration.

features.name: Agent-Level Cost Attribution in UI
features.description: Usage charts exist but don't show per-agent breakdowns. Add source_agent grouping to usage dashboard so users can see which agents burn most credits.

stories.title: Graceful budget exhaustion in autonomousExecutor — complete current action, stop loop
acceptance: When checkBudget returns allowed=false BEFORE a Claude API call, the agent loop exits cleanly (does not throw CreditExhaustedError)
The current in-flight iteration completes normally — the check only prevents the NEXT iteration from starting
The execute() return object includes a new field: `stoppedReason: 'credits_exhausted' | 'max_iterations' | null`
A user-facing message is appended to the response: 'This action was completed but the agent loop was paused — your credit balance is low.'
The existing CreditExhaustedError throw is preserved as a fallback for cases where deduction itself fails mid-action (safety net)
stories.notes: The budget check already runs at the top of each iteration (line 393). Move the check AFTER the current iteration completes its Claude call, so the in-flight action finishes. Use a flag `shouldStop` that breaks the while loop after the current tool execution completes. Do NOT change the catch block for CreditExhaustedError — keep it as a safety net for edge cases where deduction fails mid-action.

stories.title: Credit exhaustion toast notification and CopilotContext handling
acceptance: When autonomousExecutor returns stoppedReason='credits_exhausted', CopilotContext shows a toast: 'Agent paused — credit balance is low. Top up to continue.'
The toast includes an action button linking to /settings/credits (the existing credits page)
The agent response is still displayed normally — only the toast is new
No duplicate toasts if multiple agents hit the limit simultaneously (debounce 5s)
stories.notes: Use toast.warning from sonner with an action button. The CopilotContext already handles agent execution results — add a check for the new stoppedReason field.

stories.title: Add bundled_credits to Basic plan and update credit grant logic
acceptance: Migration updates subscription_plans.features JSONB for 'basic' plan to include bundled_credits: 50 (or configured amount)
handleSubscriptionCreated: grants bundled credits for BOTH 'basic' and 'pro' plans (not just pro)
handleSubscriptionUpdated: grants credits on upgrade to basic OR pro (not just pro)
handleInvoicePaid subscription_cycle renewal: refreshes credits for both basic and pro plans
update-subscription edge function: grants credits immediately on upgrade to either plan
handleSubscriptionDeleted: expire_subscription_credits already works for any plan (no change needed)
stories.notes: The webhook currently checks `planSlug === 'pro'` in 3 places. Change to check `bundledCredits > 0` instead, which is plan-agnostic. The grant_subscription_credits and expire_subscription_credits RPCs are already plan-agnostic — no changes needed there.

stories.title: Verify and fix self-serve subscription checkout flow end-to-end
acceptance: Pricing page 'Subscribe' button for Basic (£29/mo) creates a Stripe Checkout session with mode='subscription' and correct price_id
Pricing page 'Subscribe' button for Pro (£99/mo) creates a Stripe Checkout session with mode='subscription' and correct price_id
Checkout session metadata includes org_id and plan_slug for webhook to use
After successful Stripe payment, webhook creates/updates organization_subscription and grants bundled credits
User is redirected back to billing settings page with success state
If user already has an active subscription, the button shows 'Manage Plan' and links to Stripe Customer Portal
stories.notes: The create-checkout-session edge function and Pricing page were recently fixed (CPS-002 on 2026-03-03). This story verifies the complete flow rather than building from scratch. May need to add plan_slug to the checkout session metadata if missing.

stories.title: Subscription status card on BillingSettingsPage with plan details and credit allowance
acceptance: New SubscriptionStatusCard component shows: current plan name (Basic/Pro/Trial), monthly price, billing cycle dates, next renewal date
Shows bundled credits included with plan (e.g. '50 credits/month' for Basic, '250 credits/month' for Pro)
Shows subscription credit balance vs purchased credit balance separately
If trialing: shows trial end date and 'Upgrade to keep access' CTA
If cancelled: shows 'Subscription ends on [date]' with re-subscribe CTA
Uses existing useCurrentSubscription() hook — no new data fetching
stories.notes: BillingSettingsPage already renders CreditBalanceSection and TransactionHistorySection. Add SubscriptionStatusCard above the credit section. Data comes from useCurrentSubscription() and useCreditBalance() hooks which already exist.

stories.title: Plan change flow with credit impact warning
acceptance: PlanChangeModal shows current plan vs target plan comparison (price, bundled credits, features)
Upgrade path (Basic→Pro): shows 'You will receive 250 credits/month instead of 50. Prorated charge applies.'

Downgrade path (Pro→Basic): shows warning 'Your bundled credits will reduce to 50/month. Unused subscription credits are use-or-lose — they expire at period end. Your purchased credit packs are unaffected.'
Cancel path: shows 'Your subscription credits (use-or-lose) will expire on [period end]. Your purchased credit packs never expire and remain fully available.'
Confirm button calls update-subscription edge function (upgrade/downgrade) or creates Stripe portal session (cancel)
Success/error toast feedback after action completes
stories.notes: PlanChangeModal.tsx already exists. This story enhances it with credit impact warnings. The update-subscription edge function already handles upgrade/downgrade with credit granting. For cancellation, use the existing useCreatePortalSession() hook to redirect to Stripe's cancellation flow.

stories.title: Add source_agent grouping to usage breakdown chart
acceptance: UsageChart supports a new 'By Agent' tab alongside existing 'By Feature' view
Groups credit_logs by source column (user_initiated, agent_automated, sequence_step, scheduled)
For agent_automated entries, further groups by the action field to show which agent actions cost most
Shows top 5 cost-consuming actions with a 'Show all' expansion
Uses existing get_user_credit_logs RPC — adds groupBy parameter or client-side aggregation
stories.notes: credit_logs table already has source and action columns. The data exists — this is purely a UI grouping change. Prefer client-side aggregation from the existing credit_logs query rather than a new RPC to keep it simple.

parallelGroups.name: Phase 1: Graceful Degradation + Basic Credits (P1)
parallelGroups.note: Independent — fleet degradation and plan credit logic can run in parallel.

parallelGroups.name: Phase 1b: Fleet UX + Checkout Verification (P1/P2)
parallelGroups.note: CGV-002 depends on CGV-001. CGV-004 depends on CGV-003. Both can run in parallel with each other.

parallelGroups.name: Phase 2: Subscription UI (P2)
parallelGroups.note: Sequential — status card first, then plan change modal builds on it.

parallelGroups.name: Phase 3: Attribution (P3)
parallelGroups.note: Independent of all other stories. Can run anytime.

alreadyBuilt: Credit packs: CRED-001–023 (complete). FIFO deduction, auto top-up, credit menu, 20 UI components.
Credit ledger: CREDIT-001–006 (complete). creditLedger.ts, creditBudgetService.ts, autonomous executor wiring.
Stripe webhooks: subscription.created/updated/deleted, invoice.paid/failed/finalized, payment_intent.succeeded/failed — ALL handled with idempotency.
Subscription credit lifecycle: Pro plan gets 250 credits on creation, refresh on renewal (invoice.paid subscription_cycle), expire on cancellation.
Auto top-up: fire-and-forget charge creation → webhook-confirmed fulfillment (payment_intent.succeeded). 2-failure auto-disable. Monthly caps.
Budget enforcement: 80% soft-warn (blocks non-critical), hard-kill at grace threshold, fail-open on error, 60s cache.
Model router: model_config table, circuit breaker, 3 tiers (low/medium/high), 5 features, org-level overrides.
Atomic deduction: deduct_credits_fifo() with FOR UPDATE row lock. No double-spending possible.
Pricing page: Recently fixed (CPS-002, 2026-03-03) to create Stripe checkout sessions.
existingInfrastructure.doNotRebuild: All of the above is working. This plan only addresses graceful degradation, Basic plan credits, subscription UI, and agent attribution.

plan-billing-completeness-audit.json.covers: Unwired edge functions (18+ money leak), CORS fixes, margin tracking, billing analytics, activation checklist
plan-billing-completeness-audit.json.status: Planned but unstarted — should be executed alongside or before this plan
plan-billing-completeness-audit.json.recommendation: Run GAP-002/003/004 (credit deduction wiring) BEFORE this plan ships, as unwired functions will burn unmetered credits regardless of governance.

outOfScope: Wiring credit deduction to 18+ unwired edge functions — covered by plan-billing-completeness-audit.json (GAP-002/003/004/009)
Auto top-up Stripe flow changes — already webhook-confirmed, not fire-and-forget as initially thought
Budget cap daily/weekly reset logic — already implemented in credit_budget_caps migration, just needs testing
Concurrent stress testing — testing concern, not a code story
CORS fixes in Stripe functions — covered by GAP-005
Provider cost margin tracking — covered by GAP-006/007
Intelligence tier downgrade on low credits — user chose 'complete current, stop loop' over tier cascading
Invoice generation / itemised billing — handled by Stripe Customer Portal
Currency normalisation (GBP/USD) — larger refactor, out of scope

businessRules.subscriptionCredits: Use or lose. Granted at cycle start, expire at cycle end. Consumed FIRST in deduction order (before packs). Already implemented: grant_subscription_credits sets expiry, expire_subscription_credits sweeps at renewal/cancellation, deduct_credits_ordered consumes subscription credits first.
businessRules.creditPacks: Never expire. credit_packs.expires_at defaults to NULL. Consumed LAST via FIFO (bonus packs before oldest purchased). Already implemented correctly.
businessRules.deductionOrder: subscription credits → onboarding credits → credit packs (FIFO: bonus first, then oldest purchased_at)
businessRules.pricingModel: Subscription (£29/month Basic, £99/month Pro) with bundled credits + top-up credit packs

consultAnswers.pricingModel: Subscription (£29/month Basic, £99/month Pro) with bundled credits + top-up credit packs
consultAnswers.failPosture: Keep fail-open (availability over accuracy, reconcile after)
consultAnswers.fleetDegradation: Complete current action, stop loop, notify user
consultAnswers.prdScope: Both — Phase 1 hardens existing, Phase 2 builds subscription layer

summary.keyInsight: The credit system is 90% production-grade, not 'stubs only'. 37+ stories shipped across 4 major phases. The real gaps are: (1) graceful fleet degradation instead of hard-abort, (2) Basic plan bundled credits (only Pro is wired), (3) subscription management UI polish, and (4) agent-level cost attribution visibility.
summary.afterCompletion: Full hybrid SaaS + consumption model: £29/mo Basic (50 bundled credits), £99/mo Pro (250 bundled credits), credit packs for top-ups, graceful agent degradation, and per-agent usage visibility. Combined with the billing-completeness-audit plan (GAP-001–010), the billing system will be 100% complete.
summary.criticalDependency: GAP-002/003/004/009 from plan-billing-completeness-audit MUST run before or alongside — 18+ edge functions currently burn unmetered credits. No governance system matters if costs aren't being tracked.
